task: I’ve got to roll out a small process change to my team and I want the message to be clear without turning into a big speech. Can you help me draft something I could send in a quick note, plus a shorter version I could use if I need to say it out loud?

turns.agent_response:
Yes — keep it short and direct.

## Quick note
“Starting next week, we’re making a small change to how we handle this process so we can keep things moving and reduce rework. Please follow the updated steps, keep files complete, and flag anything unclear right away. If something doesn’t line up, stop and ask before it turns into cleanup later.”

## Short spoken version
“Starting next week, we’re using the updated process. Follow the steps, keep files complete, and flag issues early so we don’t create rework.”

If you want, I can make it sound:
- firmer
- more branch-specific
- more conversational

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, close. But it’s still a little generic.

Make it sound more like I’m setting the standard, not asking for cooperation. Also tie it to accuracy and turnaround, not this vague ‘reduce rework’ stuff.

Give me:
- a cleaner quick note
- a more direct spoken version
- maybe one that sounds a little firmer without being a lecture

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here are tighter versions with more authority.

## Quick note
“Starting next week, we’re moving to the updated process. This is now the standard, and I expect everyone to follow it exactly so we keep files accurate and turnaround on track. Make sure the file is complete before it moves, and flag anything unclear right away. If it doesn’t line up, stop and fix it before it leaves your desk.”

## Direct spoken version
“Starting next week, this is the process. Follow the steps, keep the file complete, and flag issues right away so we stay accurate and keep turnaround moving.”

## Firmer version
“Starting next week, we’re using the updated process as the standard. I expect it followed the same way every time so we stay accurate, keep turnaround moving, and avoid problems later. If something is off, stop and address it before it goes any further.”

If you want, I can make these even more:
- branch-manager blunt
- polished for email
- short enough for a huddle opener

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s better. Clean and straight.

The firmer one is basically what I wanted. The line about it being the standard and keeping turnaround moving works. Doesn’t sound like I’m begging anybody to buy in.

If I use it, I’d probably trim it just a hair for a quick note, but this is usable.
